roadmap.milestone: M4-VC — VC / ZKP credential exchange (the signed-VC default)
roadmap.note: The W3C Verifiable Credentials 2.0 data model + sign/verify backbone with a PLUGGABLE proof-suite seam. Build VC 2.0 credentials/presentations as queryable JSON-LD/RDF (real W3C vocab https://www.w3.org/ns/credentials/v2) via the suite RDF libs (@jeswr/fetch-rdf / @solid/object / n3.Writer — never bespoke); sign with an embedded Data Integrity proof (eddsa-rdfc-2022 / ecdsa-rdfc-2019 over RDFC-1.0 via jose/WebCrypto + rdf-canonize); verify through independent fail-closed gates (signature, expiry, not-yet-valid, issuer-binding, proof-purpose, trusted-issuer). Headline use case: a signed AgentAuthorizationCredential ('WebID X authorizes agent Y for action Z under ODRL policy P') composing with M1 (@jeswr/solid-agent-card) + M3 (@jeswr/solid-odrl). The ProofSuite interface + SuiteRegistry are the seam a BBS / JWT / SPARQ ZK-over-SPARQL proof plugs into WITHOUT touching the data model or the verify pipeline — the ZKP cryptography itself lives in @jeswr/sparq (the SPARQ agent's domain), NOT here. SEPARATE-CODEBASE — zero prod-solid-server core risk (client-side library; no networking, no server change). Supersedes/consolidates @jeswr/vc-cli + @jeswr/vc-queries (cited, not green-fielded). NEXT in M4: BBS support here (the interop floor), then the @jeswr/agent-zk-sparql wire-encoding adapter; the SERVER-SIDE delegated-token / trust-check enforcement (M5b/M5d) is CORE-PSS and is NOT built here (flagged for maintainer approval).
